question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RedisTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the `container` Mono's `.cache()` behavior, where a cached error state prevents any new Redis connection from being established, permanently blocking all subscribers from receiving messages until the service is restarted?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting `BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR` in `onBackpressureBuffer`, where a single slow subscriber on a shared topic causes the buffer to overflow, emitting an error that terminates the entire shared Flux and disconnects all other subscribers on that topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by opening subscriptions to thousands of distinct topic IDs, where each subscription creates a `ReactiveRedisMessageListenerContainer` channel listener, exhausting the Redis server's maximum number of subscribed channels and preventing legitimate subscribers from connecting?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the `computeIfAbsent` call without any synchronization guard, allowing two concurrent threads to both pass the absence check and both call `subscribe()`, creating two separate Redis channel subscriptions for the same topic that both persist indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the `Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval)` on the container Mono, where `Long.MAX_VALUE` retries with a minimum interval of 500ms means the retry loop can run for effectively infinite time, permanently consuming a scheduler thread and blocking other operations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by subscribing to a topic and sending a crafted gRPC request that causes the `TopicMessageFilter` deserialization to allocate large objects, which combined with `prefetch=48` pre-fetching causes heap exhaustion before any backpressure signal is emitted?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the absence of a circuit breaker on the `subscribe()` Flux, allowing a permanently failing Redis subscription to retry indefinitely via `Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...)`, consuming CPU and scheduler resources without ever succeeding?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: unsubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the `doOnComplete(() -> unsubscribe(topic))` callback, where completing a subscription removes the topic from `topicMessages`, and if this races with a concurrent `computeIfAbsent` call, the new entry is immediately orphaned and the old subscription is lost, causing a subscription leak?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by subscribing to a topic ID that maps to a very long channel name string via `getTopic()`, causing the `topicMessages` map keys to consume disproportionate heap memory when multiplied across thousands of entries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the `container.flatMapMany()` call, where each retry of the subscribe Flux calls `flatMapMany` again on the cached container Mono, potentially creating multiple concurrent `r.receive()` calls on the same `ReactiveRedisMessageListenerContainer` instance, causing internal state corruption?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fixed `maxBufferSize` of 16384 messages per subscriber, where subscribing to a high-volume topic with many concurrent subscribers causes 16384 * N messages to be buffered simultaneously, exhausting heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by triggering the `doOnSubscribe(s -> log.info(...))` callback thousands of times per second through rapid subscription cycling, causing the logging framework's internal queue to overflow and either drop log entries or block the calling thread?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the lack of any TTL or idle-timeout on entries in `topicMessages`, allowing stale Flux entries for topics with no active Redis publishers to accumulate indefinitely, consuming memory without ever being evicted?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by subscribing to a topic and then causing the Redis message deserialization (`messageSerializer`) to throw a runtime exception on every message, triggering the `retryWhen` loop indefinitely and consuming CPU without delivering any messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the `publishOn(Schedulers.boundedElastic(), false, listenerProperties.getPrefetch())` call, where `false` for the `delayError` parameter means errors are propagated immediately, and a crafted error in one subscriber's pipeline can propagate to terminate the shared Flux for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the `r.receive(Collections.singletonList(topic), channelSerializer, messageSerializer)` call, where subscribing to thousands of topics causes the `ReactiveRedisMessageListenerContainer` to maintain thousands of channel subscriptions, exhausting its internal connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that `topicMessages.computeIfAbsent` stores the result of `subscribe(topic)` which includes a `.share()` operator, and if the shared Flux terminates with an error, the error is cached and replayed to all future subscribers without ever being removed from the map?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the interaction between `.share()` and `retryWhen`, where a retry after all subscribers have cancelled creates a new upstream subscription but the old `.share()` operator is still referenced in `topicMessages`, causing a split-brain state with two active Redis subscriptions for the same topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by subscribing to a topic with a very small `endTime` value in `TopicMessageFilter`, causing the subscription to complete immediately and trigger `doOnComplete(() -> unsubscribe(topic))`, then immediately re-subscribing in a tight loop to repeatedly create and destroy Redis channel subscriptions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the `Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval).maxBackoff(interval.multipliedBy(4L))` configuration, where the maximum backoff of 2 seconds means a failing subscription retries at most every 2 seconds, and with thousands of concurrent failing subscriptions this creates a constant high-frequency retry storm?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the `ConcurrentHashMap` used for `topicMessages`, where concurrent insertions from many threads cause the map to resize repeatedly, triggering expensive rehashing operations that block all map operations and starve legitimate subscriber threads?
